Fixed an 11-month shipping-discount classification bug. dtc_shipping_discounts was $0 for Jun 2025 onward because Shopify started emitting shipping waivers as proper allocations (target_type=SHIPPING_LINE), and transform_daily.py was treating ALL allocations as line-item discounts. Pushed organic-certification backfill to Shopify: 63/63 ops successful (4 'usda organic' tag adds, 1 tag remove on Raspberry Mocha, 25 'USDA Organic' + 33 'Made with Organic' custom.organic metafield writes). Both signals now consistent with master.certifications canon.

Found minimal tag drift (4 missing + 1 extra) and broad metafield underuse (58 products empty); zero metafield value disagreements where set. No writes performed; backfill plan awaits Justin's call.

Pushed PD nutrition values to Shopify metafields across 96 products. 509/509 writes succeeded (178 creates, 331 updates). Remaining mismatches dropped from 821 to 343 (mostly PD-blank rows that need a Shopify->PD pull, not a push).

Audited PRODUCT DATABASE nutrition vs live Shopify metafields across 96 unique products. Wrote new 'Website Diff' sheet with 821 per-(product, nutrient) discrepancies categorized + colored. Bottom line: NOT consistent.
Computed %DV from FDA reference values for 63 PD nutrition cells with non-zero qty + missing pct. Regenerated TEA + INSTANT sheets to inherit. Existing pcts preserved (no overrides).

Normalized 113 PRODUCT DATABASE nutrition cells (caffeine unit, less-than-1g standardization, fill obvious 0% on zero-qty cells), then regenerated TEA + INSTANT COLLECTIONS sheets to inherit the cleaned data.

Fixed nutrition column population on TEA + INSTANT COLLECTIONS sheets: split PD's combined 'X g (Y%)' format into separate qty (e.g. '0g') and pct (e.g. 0.01 as decimal) cells matching COFFEE COLLECTIONS schema.

Added TEA COLLECTIONS (70 SKUs) and INSTANT COLLECTIONS (14 SKUs) sheets to Product Database - with SKUs.xlsx, mirroring COFFEE COLLECTIONS schema. Carried nutrition + ingredients from PRODUCT DATABASE rows where (family + flavor) match.

projects.name: ADA Compliance / Replo
projects.description: Website ADA compliance updates using Replo page builder modules. Almost done — finishing remaining pages.

projects.name: Agent Team Deployment
projects.description: Deploy multi-agent team with separate Discord bots. Each agent gets its own Discord bot, workspace, and isolated channel access. Orchestration rules route tasks to the right agent automatically.

projects.name: Amazon Sponsored Brand Video
projects.description: Video ad for Amazon Sponsored Brand Video placement. Script drafted, needs shot list and filming.

projects.name: Versatility Campaign (One Flavor, Four Ways)
projects.description: Paid media push telling Teeccino's versatility story — 4 hero flavors (French Roast, Adapt & Protect, Dandelion Dark Roast, Mocha) × 4 formats (Full-Grind Coffee / Tee-Bags / Maya Instant Coffee / Maya Instant Latte). 16 SKUs in scope. $15K over 31 days (2026-05-04 → 2026-06-03), Meta 80% / Google 20%. Budget reallocated from Meta COLD. KPI: account-level MER floor ≥ 6.0x. Inaugural test of AdSmith's campaign-planning skill — first Teeccino campaign tracked end-to-end as a project (brief → creative → launch → optimize → post-mortem).

projects.name: CatalogSmith Deployment (PIM Agent)
projects.description: Deploy CatalogSmith as the 7th guild agent — the Product Information Management (PIM) steward. Owns metafield/metaobject schemas, taxonomy (families, styles, tags), and product copy (titles, descriptions, SEO, flavor notes, nutrition, brew methods, ingredients). Writes catalog fields to Shopify directly; Shopkeeper retains operational writes (promos, pricing, inventory, publish). Phased rollout: 0) Bootstrap → 1) Extract live Shopify catalog into master xlsx → 2) First completeness audit → 3) Fill gaps → 4) Copy pass w/ brand voice → 5) Syndication-ready exports (Amazon, wholesale).

projects.name: CoffeeAlternatives.com Launch & Autonomous Operation
projects.description: Launch and operate CoffeeAlternatives.com — a quietly Teeccino-owned editorial site that SEO-targets AI-answer queries in the coffee-alternatives space. Full rebuild from legacy static HTML to Jekyll + GitHub Actions. BrewSmith (8th guild agent) operates autonomously: daily content generation, community nurture, SEO audits, traffic monitoring. Ownership transparent on /disclosure/; body copy editorial and balanced.

projects.name: Cost Tracking & Profit Waterfall
projects.description: Track virtual CC costs (Meta, Google, Internet Tools cards), Shopify billing, and build profit waterfall from gross to contribution margin.

projects.name: Data Pipeline Audit & Fix
projects.description: Fix timezone mismatch and status filter bugs in SKU sales scripts that cause ~9% data inflation in KPIs. Affects Daily SKU Sales report and downstream matrices.

projects.name: Influencer Gifting Program
projects.description: Scalable gifting program — send curated boxes to micro-influencers, track ROI on organic social content. Creator discovery pipeline running daily.

projects.name: Upgrade to New Customer Accounts
projects.description: Migrate Teeccino from Shopify CLASSIC customer accounts to NEW customer accounts. Primary driver: unlocks Stay.ai's Shopify Customer Account Extensions (no-code app blocks in Theme Editor → Checkout and customer accounts), giving subscribers richer in-page subscription management (skip/pause/gift/swap) without leaving /account. Secondary benefits: Shop-account integration (passwordless login, saved payment, faster checkout), official Shopify support roadmap (classic is legacy). Cost: changes the entire customer-account UX, needs coordination on email templates, any other theme touchpoints referencing /account routes, and a merchant-side switch in Shopify Admin → Settings → Customer accounts. Non-urgent — the current classic-accounts + Liquid CTA to Stay.ai portal (shipped 2026-04-17) works fine.

projects.name: New Packaging Rollover
projects.description: Full rollover of all Teeccino products to new packaging. Consolidates carousel-image-sync and website-photo-update. AssetSmith owns the master Assets library, deposits to Shopify\New Packaging Carousel\ (live destination as of 2026-05-05), AND directly pushes images to Shopify (lane transfer 2026-05-07). CatalogSmith owns titles + non-image metafields. Shopkeeper owns product/variant creation and non-image Shopify ops. 175 products tracked in New Packaging.xlsx across 7 carousel image slots each.

projects.name: PDP ATC Donation Upsell Popup
projects.description: Build a minimally-invasive, ADA-compliant upsell modal that triggers on Add-to-Cart on the new pdp-rebuild template (now live on 85 products). First upsell offer is NOT a Teeccino product — it's an NGO donation using the existing Shopify donation product that already powers the checkout donation upsell flow. Goal: add the donation variant to cart with one click, dismiss with one click, no interference with OCU / cart drawer / Stay.ai / Loox. Session-scoped suppression so repeat ATCs on the same session don't re-nag. WCAG 2.1 AA required. Justin is offline for the build — expects near-complete with a preview URL to QA and flip to live tonight.

projects.name: PDP Rebuild (Replo → Native Liquid)
projects.description: Rebuild the main Replo product detail page (template 10dc3372) as a native Liquid section in the teeccino-dawn-refresh theme. 99 products use this template. Goal: eliminate Replo dependency so ThemeSmith can directly maintain, update, and optimize PDPs. Scope is bound to live-page functionality only (see pdp-live-spec.md) — Replo-chunk features not present on the live page are dropped. Secondary: rebuild 3 other Replo templates (15 products total) after main template is complete.

projects.name: Promo Calendar Management
projects.description: Ongoing management of the Teeccino promotional calendar. Includes planning Earth Day sale, monthly FOTM changeovers, and coordinating with promo management system.

projects.name: Promo Management System
projects.description: End-to-end promo lifecycle: inventory analysis, metafield updates, discount creation/deactivation, collection management, and performance tracking.

projects.name: Reports Hub & Navigation IA
projects.description: Stand up HubSmith as the dedicated webmaster for teeccino-reports (internal GH Pages site). Solve the orphan-by-default problem: every dashboard publisher hand-edits index.html or doesn't, so new dashboards default to invisible. Solution: manifest-driven hub (pages.json), shared chrome (nav.html partial), publisher contract (register_page helper), automated hygiene (orphan + dead-link sweeps), archival policy. ~96 HTML files currently exist, ~11 are linked from the hub. After this project, every dashboard reachable in ≤2 clicks from home, every new dashboard auto-registered, stale content auto-archived.

humanActions.title: Provision GA4 Data API service-account credentials (unblocks PDP upsell funnel ingest)
humanActions.detail: DataOps stub ingest_ga4.py is in place but exits cleanly with BLOCKED status until creds exist. To unblock: (1) GCP Console -> enable Google Analytics Data API on a project of your choice; (2) create service account ga4-dataops-readonly + download JSON keyfile; (3) save the JSON to OneDrive/Apps/GoogleAnalyticsAPI/service-account.json; (4) create OneDrive/Apps/GoogleAnalyticsAPI/.env with GA4_PROPERTY_ID=<numeric id from GA4 Admin -> Property Settings> and GA4_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON=<absolute path to JSON>; (5) in GA4 (analytics.google.com) -> Admin -> Property Access Management, grant the service account email Viewer role on property G-QG7N4XBQHK. Full step-by-step in the docstring at the top of ingest_ga4.py. Once creds are in place, ping DataOps to finish wiring the runReport pull + populate the funnel section of pdp-upsell-performance.html (currently stubbed PENDING).

incidents.title: Phantom-cleanup batch renamed SKUs on 19+ active free-item / GWP / donation / gift-box products
incidents.summary: Bulk SKU rename batch of 79 SKUs across 35 "phantom" products treated status=archived/draft/unlisted as phantom-eligible. Live BXGY discounts, FOTM Free Packet flow, Stay.ai bundles, donation upsell, and ~19 active free-item / GWP / Mystery Gift / gift-box products broke. Justin caught it on the live site ~90 min after the run; Shopkeeper reverted all 79 SKUs in 30 min, 0 failures.
incidents.rootCause: AgentSmith dispatched the cleanup with insufficient safety thinking. CatalogSmith's phantom signal (duplicate-SKU + non-active-status) inverts reality at Teeccino, where status=archived/draft/unlisted is the canonical hidden-but-active state for cart-addable products. Single safety guard (skip status=active) caught 48/83 entries; the remaining 35 included ~19 active products. Free Java Packet 7976160755960 — explicitly named in Shopkeeper CLAUDE.md as the FOTM Free Packet — got renamed despite being a hard-rule ID, because the hard-rule was in markdown text, not in a machine-readable refuse-list.

criticalActiveProductsHit: Free Java Packet (FOTM Free Packet, hard-coded)
Free Teeccino Teal Tote (Earth Day GWP)
Multiple gift boxes (Father's Day, Mother's Day, Bold Brew, Fall, Pour Over)

correctiveActions.description: Build clawd/data/protected-ids.json — explicit ID-level refuse-list for hard-rule items (Free Packet, Live Theme, FOTM discounts). Mutation scripts MUST consult before write.
Add `incidents` field + dedicated incidents/ directory to Mission Control. Render in dashboard so postmortems are visible to Justin without reading inboxes.
Build shared sku_safety lib (variant_is_referenced_by_active_rule, etc.). Any bulk-mutation script MUST call this before write.
deliverables: 5-function API: variant_is_referenced_by_active_rule, product_is_referenced_by_active_rule, sku_is_used_by_multiple_products, refuse_if_protected, preflight_bulk_mutation
